disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Able to bear weight, no bony deformity/step-off, full/near-full joint movement (knee/hip) — argues against femoral fracture
trigger_question_examples: Can you bear weight and walk on it, and is there any obvious deformity or a grinding/step in the bone?
Can you move your hip and knee, even if it hurts?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I can bear weight and walk with a limp, no obvious deformity, and I can move my hip and knee.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Compartment soft/compressible, pain NOT out of proportion, no severe tense woody swelling, no numbness/tingling, warm foot with normal colour — argues against compartment syndrome/vascular injury
trigger_question_examples: Is the thigh becoming tensely, woodenly swollen with pain far worse than expected, or any numbness/tingling?
Is your foot warm with normal colour and feeling, and can you move your toes?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: The pain's manageable, the thigh isn't rock-hard tense, no numbness, and my foot is warm with normal feeling and movement.
